enriched_points.expanded_content: ZT values of oxide-based thermoelectric materials range from 0.2 to 0.8, highlighting their potential for efficient thermoelectric applications (Source 1).
Thermoelectric material efficiency varies significantly with ZT values; optimal performance occurs at higher temperature differentials (ΔT), achieving up to 17.1% efficiency at 860 K (Source 5).
Nanocomposites enhance thermoelectric performance by optimizing ZT through improved electrical conductivity and reduced thermal conductivity, leading to higher energy conversion efficiency.
